chapter_voice_summary: BG 2.71's voice is consistent with chapter 2's didactic-corrective register. The opening 'The Sanskrit names four conditions: vihāya kāmān (desires abandoned), niḥspṛha (longing absent), nirmama (without mine), nirahaṅkāra (without I-as-doer)' is the senior-engineer-grammarian flat enumeration that mirrors the chapter's preferred treatment of technical-term-rich verses. The 'The third and fourth carry the engineering weight' is the kind of explicit scoping move the chapter rewards — it names which components carry which work. The corrective edge appears in the falsifiability clause's named failure mode ('an engineer who lets a critical service rot because it is not mine has confused nirmama with abandonment'). The 'Shipped artifact and operator identity are uncoupled. Peace, says Krishna, follows' is structural-architectural — a senior compression rather than aphorism. No tonal break. Pass.
